message:
Implement multi-client Pub/Sub registry in ServerState

The previous Pub/Sub implementation stored subscriptions and pending
messages inside the per-session Store, which meant only the currently
active session could receive publishes. In a real Redis server with
multiple concurrent clients this is incorrect — PUBLISH must fan out
to every subscribed client regardless of which session is executing.

Add global Pub/Sub state to ServerState:
- pubsub_channel_subs / pubsub_pattern_subs / pubsub_shard_subs:
  channel -> set<client_id> maps for tracking who is subscribed
- pubsub_outbox: per-client message queue for async delivery

Add full Pub/Sub API surface on Runtime:
- pubsub_subscribe / pubsub_unsubscribe (channels)
- pubsub_psubscribe / pubsub_punsubscribe (patterns)
- pubsub_ssubscribe / pubsub_sunsubscribe (shard channels)
- pubsub_publish: fans out Message to channel subs + PMessage to
  matching pattern subs
- pubsub_spublish: fans out SMessage to shard channel subs
- pubsub_cleanup_client: removes all subs + outbox on disconnect
- drain_pubsub_for_client / pubsub_clients_with_pending: helpers
  for the server event loop to deliver queued messages

The existing drain_pending_pubsub() is updated to merge legacy
per-Store messages with the new global outbox for backwards compat
with single-session tests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
